perturbation.original_text: 3.2.3 You shall, at our option, execute our then-current form of Master Franchise Agreement and any addenda thereto for the renewal term, which renewal agreement shall supersede this Agreement in all respects, and the terms of which, including, without limitation, continuing fees payable to us, may differ materially and be less advantageous to you than the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2.3 You shall, at our option, execute our then-current form of Master Franchise Agreement and any addenda thereto for the renewal term, which renewal agreement shall mirror this Agreement in all respects, and the terms of which, including, without limitation, continuing fees payable to us, shall be identical and as advantageous to you as the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the renewal agreement 'may differ materially and be less advantageous'. The modified text contradicts this by stating the renewal agreement 'shall be identical and as advantageous'. This creates uncertainty about the terms of renewal, making it unclear whether the franchisee will face more onerous conditions upon renewal, contradicting the original expectation set by the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2.3 You shall, at our option, execute our then-current form of Master Franchise Agreement and any addenda thereto for the renewal term, which renewal agreement shall mirror this Agreement in all respects, and the terms of which, including, without limitation, continuing fees payable to us, shall be identical and as advantageous to you as the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and, by extension, itself as it is also the contradicted text. The original text states the renewal agreement 'may differ materially and be less advantageous,' while the changed text says it 'shall be identical and as advantageous.' This creates a clear and irreconcilable contradiction regarding the terms of the renewal, as the agreement cannot simultaneously be both potentially disadvantageous and strictly identical and advantageous.

perturbation.original_text: 5.14 Solicitation Advertising You shall conduct advertising to solicit Applicants for the purchase of Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory ("Solicitation Advertising"). You shall expend not less than $5,000 each month on such Solicitation Advertising. To protect the Proprietary Marks and our goodwill in the industry, you must submit samples of all proposed Solicitation Advertising materials to us at least fifteen (15) days before any use of the same. If we do not disapprove the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials within fifteen (15) days after receipt of the same, you may use the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials as submitted to us; provided, however, that if such Solicitation Advertising is required to be submitted to a government agency, you shall so submit such materials to the applicable government agency and shall not use such materials until the materials are approved or disapproved or if the use of the materials otherwise become permissible under law, such as if notice of disapproval is not received from a governmental agency within a stated period of time prescribed by law. We may, at any time after you begin to use the Solicitation Advertising materials, prohibit further use of the same, effective upon your receipt of written notice from us to do so.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.14 Solicitation Advertising You shall conduct advertising to solicit Applicants for the purchase of Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory ("Solicitation Advertising"). You are not required to expend any money on such Solicitation Advertising, unless the Franchisor approves the expense, in writing. To protect the Proprietary Marks and our goodwill in the industry, you must submit samples of all proposed Solicitation Advertising materials to us at least fifteen (15) days before any use of the same. If we do not disapprove the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials within fifteen (15) days after receipt of the same, you may use the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials as submitted to us; provided, however, that if such Solicitation Advertising is required to be submitted to a government agency, you shall so submit such materials to the applicable government agency and shall not use such materials until the materials are approved or disapproved or if the use of the materials otherwise become permissible under law, such as if notice of disapproval is not received from a governmental agency within a stated period of time prescribed by law. We may, at any time after you begin to use the Solicitation Advertising materials, prohibit further use of the same, effective upon your receipt of written notice from us to do so.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states 'You shall expend not less than $5,000 each month on such Solicitation Advertising'. The modified text contradicts this by saying 'You are not required to expend any money on such Solicitation Advertising, unless the Franchisor approves the expense, in writing'. This creates confusion regarding the franchisee's obligation to spend money on advertising.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.14 Solicitation Advertising You shall conduct advertising to solicit Applicants for the purchase of Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory ("Solicitation Advertising"). You are not required to expend any money on such Solicitation Advertising, unless the Franchisor approves the expense, in writing. To protect the Proprietary Marks and our goodwill in the industry, you must submit samples of all proposed Solicitation Advertising materials to us at least fifteen (15) days before any use of the same. If we do not disapprove the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials within fifteen (15) days after receipt of the same, you may use the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials as submitted to us; provided, however, that if such Solicitation Advertising is required to be submitted to a government agency, you shall so submit such materials to the applicable government agency and shall not use such materials until the materials are approved or disapproved or if the use of the materials otherwise become permissible under law, such as if notice of disapproval is not received from a governmental agency within a stated period of time prescribed by law. We may, at any time after you begin to use the Solicitation Advertising materials, prohibit further use of the same, effective upon your receipt of written notice from us to do so.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a minimum $5,000 monthly spend on advertising, while the changed text eliminates this requirement, stating no expenditure is required unless the Franchisor approves. This direct conflict creates an in-text contradiction as the franchisee's obligation regarding advertising expenditure is rendered ambiguous and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 Form of Unit Franchise Disclosure Document and Unit Franchise Agreement All Unit Franchise Disclosure Documents and Unit Franchise Agreements utilized by you with Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory shall be in substantially the form of our then-current Unit Franchise Disclosure Document and Unit Franchise Agreement, which shall be prepared by you in accordance with the provisions of this Section and those of Section 5.1, and shall be reviewed and approved by us or our counsel. You shall not use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document or Unit Franchise Agreement that we or our counsel have disapproved. You shall not use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document that has not been registered in any registration state applicable to the Master Territory. You and we acknowledge and agree that we are a third-party beneficiary to all Unit Franchise Agreements between you and Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory, and that we shall have the right to assume any of your responsibilities, duties or functions under such Unit Franchise Agreements in the event that this Agreement expires or is terminated for any reason. You shall include in the standard Unit Franchise Agreement used by you a provision which states that we are a third-party beneficiary to the Unit Franchise Agreement and are entitled to the rights granted in this Section 13. We shall have the right, but not the obligation, to enforce any provision of any Unit Franchise Agreement if you fail to properly and promptly do so. You shall not terminate any Unit Franchisee without our prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 Form of Unit Franchise Disclosure Document and Unit Franchise Agreement All Unit Franchise Disclosure Documents and Unit Franchise Agreements utilized by you with Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory shall be in substantially the form of your then-current Unit Franchise Disclosure Document and Unit Franchise Agreement, which shall be prepared by you in accordance with the provisions of this Section and those of Section 5.1, and will not be reviewed nor approved by us or our counsel. You may use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document or Unit Franchise Agreement that we or our counsel have disapproved. You may use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document that has not been registered in any registration state applicable to the Master Territory. You and we acknowledge and agree that we are a third-party beneficiary to all Unit Franchise Agreements between you and Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory, and that we shall have the right to assume any of your responsibilities, duties or functions under such Unit Franchise Agreements in the event that this Agreement expires or is terminated for any reason. You shall include in the standard Unit Franchise Agreement used by you a provision which states that we are a third-party beneficiary to the Unit Franchise Agreement and are entitled to the rights granted in this Section 13. We shall have the right, but not the obligation, to enforce any provision of any Unit Franchise Agreement if you fail to properly and promptly do so. You shall not terminate any Unit Franchisee without our prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the 'Unit Franchise Disclosure Documents and Unit Franchise Agreements...shall be reviewed and approved by us'. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating that these documents 'will not be reviewed nor approved by us'. Furthermore the original text states 'You shall not use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document or Unit Franchise Agreement that we or our counsel have disapproved', while the changed text says 'You may use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document or Unit Franchise Agreement that we or our counsel have disapproved', finally, the original text also states 'You shall not use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document that has not been registered in any registration state applicable to the Master Territory.', in contrast with the perturbation that says 'You may use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document that has not been registered in any registration state applicable to the Master Territory.'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.1 Form of Unit Franchise Disclosure Document and Unit Franchise Agreement All Unit Franchise Disclosure Documents and Unit Franchise Agreements utilized by you with Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory shall be in substantially the form of your then-current Unit Franchise Disclosure Document and Unit Franchise Agreement, which shall be prepared by you in accordance with the provisions of this Section and those of Section 5.1, and will not be reviewed nor approved by us or our counsel. You may use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document or Unit Franchise Agreement that we or our counsel have disapproved. You may use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document that has not been registered in any registration state applicable to the Master Territory. You and we acknowledge and agree that we are a third-party beneficiary to all Unit Franchise Agreements between you and Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory, and that we shall have the right to assume any of your responsibilities, duties or functions under such Unit Franchise Agreements in the event that this Agreement expires or is terminated for any reason. You shall include in the standard Unit Franchise Agreement used by you a provision which states that we are a third-party beneficiary to the Unit Franchise Agreement and are entitled to the rights granted in this Section 13. We shall have the right, but not the obligation, to enforce any provision of any Unit Franchise Agreement if you fail to properly and promptly do so. You shall not terminate any Unit Franchisee without our prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 13.1 introduces a direct contradiction to the original intent and wording of the same section. The original text states that the Unit Franchise Disclosure Documents and Unit Franchise Agreements 'shall be reviewed and approved by us', while the changed text states that they 'will not be reviewed nor approved by us'. Furthermore, it originally stated 'You shall not use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document or Unit Franchise Agreement that we or our counsel have disapproved' but it now states 'You may use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document or Unit Franchise Agreement that we or our counsel have disapproved' and lastly, it originally states 'You shall not use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document that has not been registered in any registration state applicable to the Master Territory.', in contrast with the perturbation that says 'You may use any Unit Franchise Disclosure Document that has not been registered in any registration state applicable to the Master Territory.'. Since the contradicted text is located in the same place as the changed text, this creates a clear in-text contradiction according to the structural flaw definition.
